Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Implement distributed web crawler with 6 microservices

Java 21 / Spring Boot 3.2 / Kafka-based pipeline:
- Seed URL Controller: REST API for submitting crawl seeds
- URL Frontier: politeness-enforced, disk-backed URL queue
- HTML Downloader (x3): virtual thread downloads with robots.txt
- Link Extractor (x2): Jsoup-based link parsing
- URL Filter: extension/domain/depth filtering
- URL Seen: Bloom filter + PostgreSQL deduplication

Includes Docker Compose (14 containers), Prometheus/Grafana
monitoring, unit tests, README, and draw.io architecture diagram.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

opus-2026-03-06-S11b: eigenvalue ± pairing proved via signed reversal

- PROVED: eigenvalues of M(T_full_n) come in ± pairs
- Anti-similarity: R M R^{-1} = -M at even n (R = signed reversal)
- Similarity: R M R^{-1} = +M at odd n (with λ=1 unpaired eigenvalue)
- Char poly: only even powers of λ after factoring out (λ-1) at odd n
- tr(M^{2k+1}) = 1 at odd n, = 0 at even n (all k)
- Connects to even cycle vanishing: reversal symmetry creates cancellation

El JOIN con wpyz_wc_orders_meta puede producir múltiples filas por pedido

commit.message:
fix(autotuner): skip TALL-masks for orthogonal weighted_average merges

TALL-masks classifies most positions as "selfish" for orthogonal LoRAs
(each dominates independent positions) and adds them back at full
strength after the merge, bypassing weighted_average's /N normalization.
This effectively converts weighted_average into weighted_sum (~Nx energy),
causing a residual "burned" look even after the SLERP fix.

Force standard quality (no TALL-masks, no DO-orthogonalize) for
orthogonal weighted_average prefixes since the LoRAs are already
independent and don't need conflict-resolution preprocessing.

commit.message:
Fix ticket→conversation linking: pass conversation_id through full flow

Widget now stores conversation_id from each chat response and includes it
in escalation calls. This ensures every new ticket has the correct
conversation_id stored in Supabase from the start.

Vault fallback logic tightened: removed name-based matching (too
unreliable — any visitor named John Smith could match wrong conversation)
and narrowed time-proximity window from 24h to 2h.

fix(auth): replace O(n) bcrypt scan with SHA256 indexed lookup for API key auth

287 active keys × ~50ms bcrypt = ~14s per authenticated request.
Add key_sha256 column with unique index for O(1) lookup.
Existing keys lazy-backfill on first use, no re-registration needed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(test): use relative import in defineCodeJudge fixture for worktree compatibility (#450)

The fixture imported from '@agentv/eval' which relies on workspace
symlinks in node_modules. In git worktrees, these symlinks may not be
set up, causing the test to fail with an unresolved import. Using a
relative path to the eval package source avoids workspace resolution.

Closes #449

for draft invoices. Added integration test verifying receipt movement
and stock level creation. Added E2E test for the receive flow.

commit.message:
Fix WhatsApp AI creating duplicate orders when customer responds to COD confirmation

Root cause: When a customer has WhatsApp Business with auto-replies, the auto-reply
message (e.g., "Gracias por contactarnos...") arrives before the actual "SI" response.
The COD flow marked this as 'needs_attention', then when "SI" arrived, the status was
no longer 'pending' so the COD flow skipped it, re-enabled AI, and AI created a new order.

Three fixes applied:
1. Auto-reply detection: Skip automated business replies entirely before they can
   corrupt COD confirmation status (pattern matching common auto-reply phrases)
2. Extended COD check: Also handle 'needs_attention' status for confirmation detection,
   so even if an auto-reply slipped through, "SI" still gets caught as a confirmation
3. Safer AI re-enable: Removed 'needs_attention' from AI re-enable conditions since
   it means human review is needed, not that AI should take over

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Move multi-step training into TrainingConfig with per-step IS correction (#39)

## Summary
- move multi-step training controls (`steps_per_batch`,
`feedback_repetitions`) from eval-owned settings into `TrainingConfig`
- remove eval-side sub-step loop and pass typed `training` config
through `FeedbackItem` in each `/v1/feedback` request
- execute multi-step updates inside training engines (local/modal +
tinker)
- recompute behavior-policy logprobs after each optimizer step for
off-policy importance reweighting
- include engine metadata (`steps_per_batch_applied`, per-step metrics)
and wire eval `sub_step_count` to that metadata
- update eval Hydra schema/config/docs and related tests

## Key Implementation Notes
- added strict `TrainingConfig` fields:
  - `steps_per_batch`
  - `feedback_repetitions`
- introduced Hydra-safe `EvalTrainingConfig` and convert to runtime
`TrainingConfig` in `build_harness_config`
- tinker engine now refreshes student logprobs between steps using
`save_weights_and_get_sampling_client_async`

## Validation
- `uv run ruff check claas/ tests/ --fix`
- `uv run pytest tests/ -q -m "not integration"`
  - result: `109 passed, 26 skipped, 5 deselected`
- `uv run ty check`
- unresolved-import diagnostics for heavy runtime deps (`torch`,
`tinker`, `transformers`) are expected in this environment

commit.message:
fix(admin-graph): remove Hive Writer, external providers go direct to DataLake

Architecture fix: External APIs and Blockchain nodes write directly to
tier_a — they don't go through a separate Hive Writer node. Only the
Kafka path (exchanges → ingestors → Kafka → writer) uses the Parquet Writer.

Also: better spacing — transformers get 1.1x gap to avoid overlap,
exchanges centered at x=0, consistent vertical rhythm.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
